article.title: Uber Machine coding round
article.content: I have my uber machine coding round scheduled next week. Is machine coding rounds same as LLD? am I suppose to code it out or pseudo codes will be fine? Can anyone please help me on what to prepare for this. Thanks in advance

post.content: I think working code is expected
No you are supposed to write the code for that and make sure it\'s working. kinda similar to LLD. less design more code.

It\'s essentially an LLD round where you are required to write fully working code, so pseudo-code won\'t cut it. You will likely be given a problem (like a Parking Lot or Splitwise) and expected to design the classes and implement the logic so that it actually runs against test cases within the time limit. While getting the correct output is necessary, they place a huge emphasis on code quality, modularity, and SOLID principles to see if your design is extensible, so make sure to structure it cleanly rather than just writing a script.
